checks: Player name labels are visibly smaller than the stick figure character and do not overlap with any part of the character sprite (head, body, arms, or legs)
Player name labels are positioned below the stick figure's feet (below the leg endpoints), with clear vertical gap between the legs and the text
A text input field and 'Set' button are visible near the top of the page, styled with dark background and monospace font matching the game's theme
Typing in the nickname input field does NOT cause the player to move (WASD keys are suppressed while the input is focused)
Pressing H while the nickname input is focused does NOT open the controls dialog
Entering a nickname in the input and clicking 'Set' (or pressing Enter) changes the player's displayed name label in the game
The custom nickname is visible to all players — if a second browser tab connects, it sees the first player's custom nickname
Nicknames longer than 16 characters are truncated to 16 characters
Empty or whitespace-only nicknames fall back to the default 'Player N' format
The winner announcement at round end displays the custom nickname (e.g., 'CustomName wins!' instead of 'Player 1 wins!')
All tests pass when running npm test, including new nickname validation tests
